title: torilis infesta subsp. purpurea
intro: Torilis infesta subsp. purpurea, also known as Hedge Parsley or Purple Hog's Fennel, is a plant species in the carrot family (Apiaceae) that is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Torilis infesta subsp. purpurea include its ability to relieve digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in traditional remedies for its antioxidant and anti-bacterial properties.
